title: Protect the Authenticity of Communications Sessions
description: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.

checks.name: SMB Client Signing is Required
checks.purpose: Verify LanmanWorkstation RequireSecuritySignature = 1 to authenticate SMB sessions and prevent man-in-the-middle and relay attacks

checks.name: TLS Certificate Validation is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify certificate revocation checking is not disabled, ensuring TLS sessions are authenticated against valid, non-revoked certificates

checks.name: Session Authenticity Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify organizational policy requires mutual authentication for all communications sessions handling CUI â€” policy review only

checks.name: SMB Server Signing is Required
checks.purpose: Verify LanmanServer RequireSecuritySignature = 1 to mandate SMB signing on the server side, authenticating all file sharing sessions

checks.name: LDAP Server Signing is Required
checks.purpose: Verify LDAPServerIntegrity = 2 to require LDAP signing, protecting the authenticity of directory service communications

checks.name: TLS Certificate Validation is Enabled
checks.purpose: Confirm TLS certificate revocation checking is not disabled on the server

checks.name: Kerberos Authentication is in Use
checks.purpose: Verify the server is domain-joined to confirm Kerberos mutual authentication is available for all domain communications sessions

checks.name: SSH HostbasedAuthentication is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify SSH HostbasedAuthentication is no, preventing weaker host-based trust that could undermine session authenticity

checks.name: SSH StrictHostKeyChecking is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify StrictHostKeyChecking = yes in /etc/ssh/ssh_config to enforce host key verification on outbound SSH sessions, preventing connection to impersonated hosts

checks.name: Session Authenticity Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify organizational policy requires authentication for all communications sessions â€” policy review only

checks.name: SSH HostbasedAuthentication is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify SSH HostbasedAuthentication is no, preventing weaker host-based trust that could undermine session authenticity

checks.name: SSH StrictHostKeyChecking is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify StrictHostKeyChecking = yes in /etc/ssh/ssh_config to enforce host key verification on outbound SSH sessions, preventing connection to impersonated hosts

checks.name: Session Authenticity Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify organizational policy requires authentication for all communications sessions â€” policy review only

remediation: Enable SMB signing via GPO (require it on both client and server). Enable LDAP signing (LDAPServerIntegrity = 2). Ensure TLS certificate validation is active. On Linux, set HostbasedAuthentication no and StrictHostKeyChecking yes in /etc/ssh/sshd_config and ssh_config respectively, then reload sshd.
